summary.goal: Create a radically minimal, content-first interface with only essential controls — rendered markdown fills the window with standard macOS chrome
summary.description:
The interface is radically minimal — content-first with only essential controls.

## Layout
- Full window dedicated to fully rendered markdown content
- Standard macOS title bar with traffic light buttons (close/minimize/zoom)
- **Document proxy icon** in title bar — users can drag to copy/move the file (standard macOS document behavior)
- Title bar displays filename (e.g., "README.md")
- Subtitle shows parent directory path for context
- Top-right area: one floating action button
  1. **Open With** — shows a list of available apps for the file type
- No toolbar, no sidebar, no tab bar

## HIG Compliance (Critical)
- **Do NOT add a custom Close button** — macOS provides close via traffic light buttons (top-left), Cmd+W, and title bar double-click. A custom close button violates macOS conventions.
- Use standard NSWindow chrome — traffic lights, title bar, proxy icon
- Standard window management: full screen, split view, Stage Manager, Mission Control all work via system
- Standard macOS context menus for text selection (Copy, Look Up, Translate, etc.)
- Respect system text size settings (Accessibility > Display > Text Size)

## Design Philosophy
- Preview.app-like simplicity but tuned for markdown
- FULLY RENDERED output — headings as headings, not raw ## markdown syntax
- No editing capability — purely a viewer
- No search, no bookmarks, no recent files
- Standard macOS scrolling behavior (including inertial scrolling, scroll bars)

## Open With Button
- Shows a dropdown of system-associated apps for the markdown file type
- User picks which app to open the file in
- Uses macOS Launch Services API to enumerate available apps
- Similar to Finder's Open With submenu
- Button appearance: SF Symbols icon, semi-transparent, appears on hover with smooth fade
- Position: fixed top-right, floats above content with subtle backdrop blur

## Hover Reveal Pattern
- Open With button hidden by default for maximum content focus
- Appears on mouse movement near top-right area (200ms fade-in)
- Fades out after 2s of mouse inactivity (unless hovered)
- Always visible via keyboard focus (Tab navigation)
- Touch bar button as alternative access point (if available)

## Window Behavior
- Consistent default size on every open (dynamically calculated per theme)
- Centered on the display where user's cursor is located
- No window position/size memory
- macOS standard window management (full screen, split view via system)
- Stage Manager compatible
- Supports macOS window tiling (Sequoia+)

## Keyboard Shortcuts
- Cmd+W: Close window
- Cmd+Q: Quit app
- Cmd+,: Settings/Preferences
- Cmd+Plus/Cmd+Minus: Font size adjustment
- Cmd+0: Reset font size to default
- Cmd+C: Copy selected text
- Cmd+A: Select all text
- Space/Shift+Space: Page down/up (when no text selected)
- Home/End or Cmd+Up/Cmd+Down: Scroll to top/bottom
- Tab: Cycle focus between Open With button and content area
- Standard macOS text selection and copy

## Text Interaction
- Text is fully selectable (not an image render)
- Standard macOS text cursor when hovering text
- Pointer cursor when hovering links
- Right-click context menu: Copy, Look Up, Search with Google, Share
- Links open in default browser on click
- Cmd+click for link behavior matches system preference

acceptance_criteria.description: Only Open With button visible as custom UI element (no custom Close button)
Open With button shows system-associated apps using Launch Services API
Consistent default window size based on theme font metrics
Standard macOS shortcuts (Cmd+W, Cmd+Q, Cmd+,) work correctly
Document proxy icon in title bar allows dragging file to other apps or Finder
Open With button uses hover-reveal pattern with fade animation (respects Reduce Motion)

observations: NSWindow with standard chrome — traffic lights, title bar, proxy icon
Launch Services API for Open With dropdown
Stage Manager and window tiling (Sequoia+) compatible
Open With button: SF Symbols icon, semi-transparent, hover-reveal
200ms fade-in on mouse near top-right, 2s fade-out on inactivity
FULLY RENDERED output, not raw markdown
Standard macOS text selection, context menus, inertial scrolling
HIG compliance critical — no custom Close button

designer.layout: Full window for content, no toolbar/sidebar/tabs
designer.title_bar: Filename + parent directory path + proxy icon
designer.open_with_button: SF Symbols, semi-transparent, hover-reveal, top-right with backdrop blur
designer.hig_compliance: Standard NSWindow chrome, no custom close button

steps.title: Create Open With floating button with hover-reveal behavior
steps.description: Create a floating button in the top-right area of the window that appears on mouse proximity. Use an NSView overlay positioned above the scroll view. The button uses an SF Symbols icon (e.g., arrow.up.forward.app or square.and.arrow.up) with semi-transparent background and subtle backdrop blur (NSVisualEffectView). Hidden by default (alpha 0). On mouse movement within ~60pt of the button area, fade in over 200ms. Fade out after 2s of mouse inactivity (unless mouse is directly over the button). Respect Reduce Motion: instant show/hide instead of fade. Always visible when focused via keyboard (Tab).

acceptance_criteria.description: Button positioned top-right, floating above content
SF Symbols icon with semi-transparent background and backdrop blur
Hidden by default, fades in on mouse proximity (200ms)
Fades out after 2s inactivity unless hovered
Instant show/hide when Reduce Motion is enabled
Visible when focused via Tab key

steps.title: Implement Open With dropdown using Launch Services API
steps.description: When the Open With button is clicked, query macOS for all applications that can handle the current file's UTI using NSWorkspace.urlsForApplications(toOpen:). Present the results in an NSMenu or NSPopover showing each app's name and icon. When the user selects an app, open the file in that app using NSWorkspace.open([url], withApplicationAt: appURL, configuration: config). Sort apps alphabetically. Show the app bundle icon next to each name.

acceptance_criteria.description: Dropdown lists all system-registered apps for markdown files
Each app shows its name and icon
Selecting an app opens the current file in that app
Apps sorted alphabetically
Current viewer window remains open after opening in another app

architecture.presentation: NSMenu attached to button, or NSPopover with list

steps.title: Implement font size adjustment (Cmd+Plus/Minus/0)
steps.description: Handle Cmd+Plus (increase), Cmd+Minus (decrease), and Cmd+0 (reset) for font size adjustment. Adjustments change the font size by 1pt per step. The adjustment is persisted (via ThemeManager) and applies to all open windows. When font size changes, trigger a pipeline re-render for all windows with the new size. Window does NOT resize — content reflows within existing dimensions. Set min font size to 9pt and max to 36pt.

acceptance_criteria.description: Cmd+Plus increases font size by 1pt
Cmd+Minus decreases font size by 1pt
Cmd+0 resets font size to theme default (13pt)
Font size clamped between 9pt and 36pt
Change applies to all open windows immediately
Window does not resize — content reflows

architecture.key_bindings: Menu items or NSResponder keyDown handling
architecture.re_render: Font size change triggers pipeline re-render for all windows

steps.title: Implement text selection and context menu
steps.description: Ensure the NSTextView properly supports text selection and the standard macOS context menu. NSTextView provides this largely out of the box, but verify: (1) text is selectable with standard click-drag and Cmd+A; (2) right-click shows context menu with Copy, Look Up, Search with Google, Share; (3) Cmd+C copies selected text to clipboard; (4) cursor is IBeam when hovering text, pointing hand when hovering links. Remove any editing-related context menu items (Paste, Cut, etc.) since this is a viewer.

acceptance_criteria.description: Text selectable via click-drag and Cmd+A
Right-click context menu shows Copy, Look Up, Share (no Paste/Cut)
Cmd+C copies selected text
IBeam cursor on text, pointing hand on links

architecture.context_menu: Override menu(for:) to remove editing items
architecture.cursor: NSTextView handles cursor changes automatically for non-editable text

steps.title: Implement scroll behavior with keyboard shortcuts
steps.description: Ensure standard macOS scrolling works correctly: inertial scrolling, scroll bars (auto-hidden or always visible per system preference), Space/Shift+Space for page down/up (when no text is selected), Home/End or Cmd+Up/Cmd+Down for scroll to top/bottom. The NSScrollView handles most of this natively. Verify that Space key is intercepted for scrolling only when no text selection exists (otherwise it might conflict with text view behavior).

acceptance_criteria.description: Inertial scrolling works naturally (NSScrollView default)
Space/Shift+Space scrolls page down/up when no selection
Cmd+Up/Cmd+Down scrolls to top/bottom
Home/End scrolls to top/bottom
Scroll bars respect system preference (auto-hide or always visible)

architecture.space_scroll: Override keyDown in NSTextView to handle Space when no selection
